summary: Central-ledger promotion rides the same temp-worktree commit as canonical audit persistence (RML-CONTRACT-001, proven by real-git integration test), entry_key dedupe holds against actually-pushed ledger bytes (RML-CONTRACT-002), corrupt central ledger fails closed without overwrite (RML-CONTRACT-004), unclassified_count explicit in JSON and text render paths (RML-CONTRACT-005), --gate-roi opt-in and backward compatible. 11/11 targeted tests pass, non-tautological. Two low findings recorded as followups: integration test mirrors persistCanonicalAuditToBase staging instead of calling it; local ledger never pruned after promotion.

inspection.summary: Read src/gate-outcome-ledger.js in full, src/merge-manager.js persistence wiring (355-425, 955-1095), src/usage-report.js gate-roi paths, src/cli.js flag wiring, central ledger seed, both test files in full; independently ran node --test on both ledger test files (11/11 pass) from the worktree; tailed /tmp/vibepro-rml-fulltest.log confirming no failures.
inspection.evidence: node --test test/gate-outcome-ledger-central-promotion.test.js test/gate-outcome-ledger-promotion-integration.test.js (11/11 pass, run by reviewer); /tmp/vibepro-rml-fulltest.log tail

judgment_delta: initial: promotion might create a follow-up commit or silently overwrite a corrupt central ledger -> final: pass, because stagePaths only includes the central ledger when status=promoted within the same audit-persistence commit, failed parse surfaces as merge warning without overwrite, and the real-git integration tests would fail against any pre-fix implementation
